stories.title: Quantum computing: Laser-optical system offers full control over 2,000 trapped Rydberg atoms
stories.desc: Fraunhofer ILT in Aachen has developed a highly complex laser-optical system for a quantum computer currently under construction at the 5th Institute of Physics at the University of Stuttgart. This system enables 2,000 Rydberg atoms to be positioned with submicrometer precision in the computer's…

stories.title: AI just supercharged the race to find room temperature superconductors
stories.desc: Scientists have combined machine learning with quantum physics to discover two new superconductors and create a much faster way to search for many more. The technique could bring researchers significantly closer to the long-sought goal of a room-temperature superconductor.

stories.title: Earth's deepest rocks help define upper limit for viscosity beyond which materials effectively become rigid
stories.desc: Viscosity is one of the most fundamental physical properties used to describe how materials flow. It governs the movement of liquids, molten rocks and even slowly deforming regions deep inside the Earth. While scientists have long studied materials with low or moderate viscosities, a simple but…

stories.title: Detecting neutron sources by borrowing inference tools from cosmology
stories.desc: Neutron sources can be directly identified from measured spectra rather than proxies using inference tools adapted from cosmology, according to a University of Michigan Engineering study published in Physical Review Applied. The method can improve nuclear security by helping intercept materials at…

stories.title: Researchers Reveal the Power of ‘Quantum Proofs’
stories.desc: When checking that solutions to certain problems are correct, it turns out, you can’t get around the inherent complexity of the quantum world. The post Researchers Reveal the Power of ‘Quantum Proofs’ first appeared on Quanta Magazine

stories.title: Bioinspired strategy creates complex 3D curved structures via programmed shrinkage
stories.desc: The shape of biological structures, ranging from flower petals to the limbs or organs of animals, is often naturally best suited for performing specific functions. Biological structures also often present curved surfaces with specific functional advantages, such as facilitating the drainage of…

stories.title: Reconnecting to the Lunar Trailblazer with Light
stories.desc: Learning from the attempted rescue of the Lunar Trailblazer: how ground-based optical observations and laboratory measurements can improve recovery of the attitude of a spacecraft lost in space.

stories.title: The World Cup From 250 Miles Up
stories.desc: Over the years, astronauts aboard the International Space Station have photographed several of the cities hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post The World Cup From 250 Miles Up appeared first on NASA Science .
